response:
The main cheaper alternatives to PagerDuty are **TaskCall, All Quiet, Xurrent, Better Uptime, OneUptime**, and several others like **Grafana OnCall, ilert, ZenDuty, and incident.io**, many of which offer similar incident management features at significantly lower per‑user prices.[1][2][3][4][5][6]

Below is a concise, pricing‑focused overview so you can quickly shortlist options.

---

### 1. Very budget‑friendly incident management tools

**TaskCall** – “50% cheaper” positioning  
- **Pricing:** From **$19/user/month** vs PagerDuty Business at ~$41/user/month (example in their comparison).[1]  
- **Key features:** On‑call scheduling, automated escalations, real‑time alerts, live call routing, stakeholder communication; **unlimited alerts** on paid plans.[1]  
- **Free tier:** Up to **10 users** with core incident features, aimed at small teams.[1]  
- Best if you want **PagerDuty‑like features at about half the price**.

**All Quiet**  
- **Pricing:** From **$4.99/user/month (Standard)** and **$9.99/user/month (Pro)**.[2]  
- They compare a typical PagerDuty Business setup (~$4,900/month) against All Quiet Pro (~$999/month), claiming up to **80% savings**.[2]  
- **Key features:** Flexible on‑call scheduling, smart alert routing, incident tracking, observability integrations, ISO 27001 security, transparent pricing without hidden costs.[2]  
- Best if you want **enterprise‑grade on‑call with very low per‑user cost and predictable billing**.

**Xurrent IMR**  
- **Pricing:** **$5/user/month (Starter)** and **$14/user/month (Growth)**.[3]  
- Markets itself as giving **“10x savings”** compared to PagerDuty, aiming at fast‑growing teams.[3]  
- **Key features:** Dynamic escalations and alerts, on‑call schedule management, post‑mortems, SRE practice support.[3]  
- Best if you want **cheap per‑seat pricing plus structured incident/post‑mortem workflow**.

---

### 2. Cheaper full‑stack / observability‑plus‑on‑call options

**OneUptime** (open source & SaaS)  
- **Pricing:**  
  - **Self‑host:** Free open‑source (your infra/ops cost).  
  - **Cloud:** Paid, typically still competitive vs PagerDuty.[5]  
- **Key features:** Monitoring, status pages, on‑call scheduling, incident management, logs, metrics, traces — all in one platform.[5]  
- Best if you want **a unified observability + incident platform** and/or **open source** with the option to self‑host.

**Better Uptime** (mentioned in Pragmatic Engineer list)[4]  
- Combines uptime monitoring, incident alerting, and status pages in one tool, typically at **lower price points** than a PD + separate monitoring + status page stack.[4]  
- Best if you want **monitoring + on‑call + status pages in a single cheaper product**.

**Grafana OnCall**  
- On‑call management integrated into the Grafana ecosystem, designed to be “easy‑to‑use” and reduce toil for engineers.[4]  
- Often attractive cost‑wise if you already use Grafana stack (some tiers are included with hosted plans, others can be self‑hosted).

---

### 3. Other notable commercial alternatives (often cheaper at similar feature levels)

From the curated list of 20+ alternatives by The Pragmatic Engineer:[4][6]

- **incident.io** – Incident response + on‑call + status pages, very Slack‑centric incident handling.[4]  
- **FireHydrant** – End‑to‑end incident process tooling.[4]  
- **ZenDuty** – End‑to‑end alerting, on‑call, response orchestration.[4]  
- **ilert** – On‑call, incident response, and status pages in one app.[4]  
- **SquadCast** – Reliability workflows, on‑call, incident management.[4]  
- **OnPage, AlertOps, Rootly, Blameless, xMatters** – Each with different emphases (automation, Slack‑native, enterprise workflow, etc.).[4]  

Most of these position themselves as **more modern and/or cheaper** than PagerDuty for similar use cases.[4][6]

---
